about: Located on the steep skirts of the castle, almost leaning against the eastern walls, the Church of the Virgin Mary (known locally as the Red Church) is a Syriac heritage site dating back to 179 AD, making it one of the oldest surviving sanctuaries in Anatolia. Interestingly built directly over hidden caves and niches where idols were kept during the pre-Christian pagan era, the structure reflects Harput's multi-religious history.
